matches.x: Problems of chronology arise here. How much time passed from Saul's anointing as a young man, who was out seeking his father's lost asses (1 Samuel 9:15-20), until the time of the public announcement at Mizpeh? (1 Samuel 10:17-26.) Also, how much time elapsed from that announcement until the Ammonite war and the public sustaining of King Saul at Gilgal? (1 Samuel 11:14-15). The problems arise…

"a garrison of the Philistines", a poor translation. The JPS renders it "a Philistine prefect". Thus, Jonathan only killed a local Philistine leader and not a whole garrison. "Israel also was had in abomination with the Philistines", not a good translation. A better translation would be "became malodorous/odious to the Philistines" based upon the same type of Semitism appearing in Gen. 34:30. It…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Kli Yakar on Leviticus 7:11:4
matches.x: For one who engages in the book of Genesis, it is as if he offered a burnt offering. This is the law of the burnt offering, because in it is explained the law of the burnt offering, as Abel offered a burnt offering from the firstborn of his flock (Genesis 4:4). Similarly, regarding Noah it is written, he offered burnt offerings on the altar (Genesis 8:20). And likewise with Abraham, He took the…
